extra.reasoning:
The property requires that flipping leg_swelling from false to true (all else unchanged) never lowers acuity. leg_swelling appears only in r7, which has priority 100 (dominating all other rules, max other priority is 3) and sets acuity := self_care (the LOWEST acuity). r7's condition requires palpitations, vision_loss, abdominal_pain, pallor, stiff_neck, AND leg_swelling all true.

Counterexample base input (witness_assignment, leg_swelling=false): palpitations=T, vision_loss=T, abdominal_pain=T, pallor=T, stiff_neck=T, leg_swelling=F, sweating=F, back_pain=F.

BEFORE (leg_swelling=false): r1(pallor)→emergent p3, r4(vision_loss)→emergent p3, r2/r5/r6→urgent p2 fire. r7 does not fire (leg_swelling false). Highest priority match = 3 → acuity = emergent (highest enum value).

AFTER (leg_swelling=true): same rules still fire, but now r7's full condition is satisfied. r7 has priority 100, beating everything, setting acuity := self_care. Highest priority match = 100 → acuity = self_care (lowest enum value).

Acuity drops from emergent to self_care when leg_swelling is set true, directly violating monotonicity. The witness_assignment gives the BEFORE state (leg_swelling=false); the violation occurs upon setting leg_swelling=true.
